seo.title: Fintech Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in fintech. Covers SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in fintech environments introduces significant regulatory exposure across SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and BSA/AML frameworks, where a single misconfigured extraction pipeline can trigger SEC enforcement actions or compromise cardholder data. Schema-guided LLM extraction from unstructured financial documents—such as loan applications, transaction records, and KYC filings—demands rigorous validation at every layer to ensure field accuracy, completeness, and type consistency. This checklist provides actionable controls to ensure your extraction agents meet the compliance bar required for critical financial data operations.

sections.heading: Data Ingestion & Pre-Processing Controls
sections.description: Establish secure, auditable pipelines for ingesting unstructured financial documents before they reach the LLM extraction layer.

items.label: Encrypt documents at rest and in transit
items.description: Apply AES-256 encryption for stored documents and TLS 1.3 for all data in transit, covering source files, intermediate buffers, and extracted output stores to satisfy PCI DSS Requirement 3 and 4.

items.label: Classify document sensitivity before ingestion
items.description: Tag each incoming document with a sensitivity label (e.g., PII, PAN, AML-relevant) using a pre-ingestion classifier so downstream extraction schemas apply appropriate field-level masking rules.

items.label: Validate document provenance and chain of custody
items.description: Log the source system, upload timestamp, user or service identity, and document hash (SHA-256) for every file ingested to support SOX audit trails and SEC record-keeping requirements.

items.label: Redact or tokenize PANs prior to LLM processing
items.description: Run a PCI DSS-compliant tokenization step on any document containing Primary Account Numbers before the text is passed to the LLM, ensuring raw card data never enters the model context.

items.label: Enforce data residency rules for GDPR-scoped documents
items.description: Route documents containing EU resident PII only to processing infrastructure within approved GDPR jurisdictions, and log the processing location for each document.

items.label: Apply rate limiting and anomaly detection on ingestion endpoints
items.description: Set per-client ingestion rate limits and alert on volume spikes exceeding 3x baseline to detect potential data exfiltration attempts or misconfigured upstream systems.

items.label: Scan documents for malicious content before parsing
items.description: Run antivirus and prompt-injection detection scans on all uploaded files to prevent adversarial content embedded in PDFs or images from manipulating LLM extraction behavior.

sections.heading: Schema Design & LLM Extraction Validation
sections.description: Define strict extraction schemas and implement multi-layer validation to ensure the LLM produces accurate, complete, and type-consistent structured output from financial documents.

items.label: Define and version-control extraction schemas per document type
items.description: Maintain separate JSON schemas for each document category (e.g., W-2, bank statement, loan application) in a versioned schema registry, with required fields, allowed types, and value constraints explicitly declared.

items.label: Enforce strict JSON schema validation on every LLM response
items.description: Parse and validate every LLM output against the target schema using a library such as Pydantic or AJV before the data is written to any downstream store; reject and flag non-conforming responses for human review.

items.label: Implement field-level confidence scoring
items.description: Require the LLM to emit a confidence score (0–1) alongside each extracted field and route records with any field below 0.85 confidence to a human review queue rather than auto-processing.

items.label: Validate numeric and date fields against domain constraints
items.description: Apply business-rule validators post-extraction—e.g., transaction amounts must be positive and within plausible ranges, dates must fall within expected fiscal periods—to catch hallucinated or misread values.

items.label: Check completeness against required-field lists per document type
items.description: After extraction, assert that all mandatory fields defined in the schema are present and non-null; log and escalate records with missing required fields rather than silently passing incomplete data downstream.

items.label: Run cross-field consistency checks on extracted data
items.description: Implement rule-based consistency validators—e.g., sum of line-item amounts equals reported total, account number format matches institution routing prefix—to detect extraction errors before data enters financial workflows.

items.label: Test extraction accuracy against a labeled ground-truth dataset monthly
items.description: Maintain a curated set of 200+ annotated financial documents per document type and run automated accuracy benchmarks monthly, requiring field-level F1 scores above 0.95 before any schema or model update is promoted to production.

items.label: Log full LLM input context and raw output for every extraction call
items.description: Store the exact prompt, document excerpt, model version, and raw response for each extraction call in an immutable audit log with a minimum 7-year retention period to support SOX and SEC examination requests.

sections.heading: Regulatory Compliance & Explainability Controls
sections.description: Ensure extraction outputs that influence credit, AML, or financial reporting decisions are explainable, disclaimed, and auditable in accordance with applicable regulations.

items.label: Attach explainability metadata to credit-relevant extracted fields
items.description: For any extracted field (e.g., income, debt obligations) used in credit decisioning, store a source citation—document page, bounding box or character offset, and verbatim source text—to satisfy ECOA/Regulation B adverse action explanation requirements.

items.label: Apply financial advice disclaimers to any LLM-generated summaries
items.description: If the extraction pipeline produces narrative summaries or interpretations alongside structured data, automatically append SEC-required disclaimers stating the output is not investment or financial advice.

items.label: Flag extracted data used in BSA/AML suspicious activity workflows
items.description: Tag extracted transaction fields that feed into AML screening with a provenance marker, and ensure the extraction confidence score and source document reference are included in any SAR filing support package.

items.label: Implement a human-in-the-loop review gate for high-risk extractions
items.description: Define a risk scoring function based on document type, extraction confidence, and downstream use case; require mandatory human sign-off for any extraction record scoring above the high-risk threshold before it enters a regulated workflow.

items.label: Maintain a model card and data lineage record for each extraction model version
items.description: Document training data sources, known limitations, evaluation metrics, and intended use cases for each LLM version used in extraction; update and retain these records to support SEC and OCC model risk management examinations.

items.label: Conduct quarterly bias audits on extraction accuracy across demographic segments
items.description: Analyze extraction error rates segmented by document language, institution type, and geographic region to detect systematic inaccuracies that could constitute disparate impact in credit or AML workflows.

items.label: Implement GDPR data subject access and erasure workflows for extracted PII
items.description: Build a lookup mechanism that maps extracted PII fields back to source documents and downstream stores, enabling complete erasure or export of a data subject's information within the 30-day GDPR response window.

sections.heading: Operational Security & Incident Response
sections.description: Establish monitoring, access controls, and incident response procedures to detect and contain failures in the extraction pipeline before they result in regulatory penalties or financial harm.

items.label: Enforce least-privilege access to extraction pipeline components
items.description: Grant each service account and human operator only the minimum permissions required for their role; review and recertify all access grants quarterly in alignment with SOX ITGC requirements.

items.label: Monitor extraction pipeline outputs for anomalous data patterns in real time
items.description: Deploy statistical process control monitors on extracted field distributions (e.g., sudden spike in null values, shift in amount ranges) and alert the operations team within 5 minutes of a detected anomaly.

items.label: Define and test a data breach response playbook specific to extraction pipeline failures
items.description: Document step-by-step procedures for containing, assessing, and notifying regulators of a breach involving extracted financial data, including GDPR 72-hour notification timelines and PCI DSS incident response requirements.

items.label: Implement immutable audit logging for all pipeline state changes
items.description: Write all configuration changes, model version deployments, schema updates, and manual overrides to a WORM (write-once-read-many) audit log to support SOX change management controls and SEC examination readiness.

items.label: Conduct penetration testing on extraction API endpoints semi-annually
items.description: Engage a qualified security assessor to perform penetration tests targeting the extraction API, focusing on prompt injection, data exfiltration via crafted documents, and authentication bypass, with findings remediated within 30 days.

items.label: Establish SLA-backed fallback procedures for LLM provider outages
items.description: Define a documented fallback workflow—such as routing to a secondary model provider or queuing for manual extraction—that activates automatically when the primary LLM API latency exceeds 10 seconds or error rate exceeds 5%.

items.label: Track and report extraction pipeline KPIs to compliance and risk committees monthly
items.description: Produce a monthly dashboard covering extraction accuracy rates, human review queue volumes, schema validation failure rates, and data breach near-misses for review by the Chief Compliance Officer and model risk committee.

notes.note: Any extraction pipeline that processes, stores, or transmits cardholder data—including PANs extracted from financial documents—falls within PCI DSS scope. Requirement 3.5 mandates that PANs be rendered unreadable anywhere they are stored, and Requirement 6.4 requires that all public-facing APIs (including extraction endpoints) are protected against known attacks. Tokenize PANs before LLM ingestion and conduct an annual PCI DSS assessment of the extraction environment.

If extracted financial data feeds into systems that support public company financial reporting, the extraction pipeline is subject to SOX IT General Controls. This requires documented change management procedures for schema and model updates, access control reviews, and evidence that automated extraction controls are operating effectively. Auditors will request evidence of validation logic, exception handling, and audit log integrity.

Automated extraction of EU resident personal data triggers GDPR obligations including maintaining a Record of Processing Activities (Article 30), honoring erasure requests within 30 days (Article 17), and ensuring that solely automated decisions with significant effects on individuals are subject to human review and explainability (Article 22). Map all extracted PII fields to data subject identities to enable compliant erasure and access responses.

Extracted transaction data used in AML screening must meet FinCEN's Customer Due Diligence and Suspicious Activity Reporting requirements. The accuracy and completeness of extracted fields—such as transaction amounts, counterparty names, and account identifiers—directly affects SAR filing quality. Regulators expect financial institutions to validate the reliability of automated data extraction tools used in AML workflows and to document their model risk management approach for these systems.

cta.heading: Automate Your Fintech Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your fintech data extraction agents against SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR, and BSA/AML requirements—catching schema drift, accuracy degradation, and compliance gaps before they become regulatory findings.
